task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal window shows the agent typing or executing the command '/home/ga/seiscomp/bin/scbulletin' or 'scbulletin'.

task_completion.description: Provide the correct database connection string
task_completion.visual_evidence: The command line includes the database connection argument 'mysql://sysop:sysop@localhost/seiscomp' (often with a -d flag).

task_completion.description: Set the time window for the full year 2024
task_completion.visual_evidence: The command line includes start and end time arguments corresponding to '2024-01-01 00:00:00' (or with 'T') and '2024-12-31 23:59:59'.

task_completion.description: Include phase arrivals and magnitudes in the output
task_completion.visual_evidence: The command line includes flags or arguments specifically requesting phases and magnitudes (e.g., -p, -m, --phases, --magnitudes).

task_completion.description: Export the output to the specified text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The command line redirects output (using '>' or an output flag like '-o') to '/home/ga/Desktop/noto_bulletin.txt'.

task_completion.description: Verify the bulletin file is created and contains earthquake data
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'noto_bulletin.txt' is visible on the Desktop, or terminal output (via cat, head, less) shows the file's contents, revealing formatted seismicity data (e.g., origin times, coordinates, magnitudes).

integrity.description: The agent actually used the scbulletin tool to generate the data
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal history shows the execution of the scbulletin command rather than the agent manually creating a text file and typing out fake data.

integrity.description: The agent did not copy-paste the Noto earthquake details directly into a file
integrity.visual_evidence: The file noto_bulletin.txt is generated via a database query command, not by opening a text editor (like nano or gedit) and pasting the prompt's description.

integrity.description: The output file is saved in the exact requested directory
integrity.visual_evidence: The file path used in the command or visible in the file explorer is exactly '/home/ga/Desktop/noto_bulletin.txt'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using the SeisComP 'scbulletin' CLI tool (/home/ga/seiscomp/bin/scbulletin) to export a seismicity bulletin. The database connection string is 'mysql://sysop:sysop@localhost/seiscomp'. The time window must be exactly '2024-01-01T00:00:00' to '2024-12-31T23:59:59' (formats may vary slightly, e.g., using spaces instead of T). The output must be saved to '/home/ga/Desktop/noto_bulletin.txt'. The resulting file should contain real earthquake data for the 2024 Noto Peninsula earthquake (magnitude 7.5).
